_doc: Hard gate (a): every landmark with tier='core' MUST be present in the corpus. tier='adjacent' papers are reported but do NOT block — they are model-editing/knowledge-localization work that neighbours interp rather than constituting it (Joao's call, 2026-07-21). Match by arxiv_id (authoritative: OpenAlex's arXiv landing-page mapping is reliable even on records whose title metadata is corrupted) OR normalized-title substring. is_seed=true means it anchors ingest_v3 (guaranteed present); is_seed=false tests real coverage via forward/foundational expansion.

_tiers.core: must be present — gate (a) fails if any is missing
_tiers.adjacent: reported only — model editing / knowledge localization, not interp core

landmarks.title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT
Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
Sparse Autoencoders Find Highly Interpretable Features
Learning to Generate Reviews and Discovering Sentiment
Understanding intermediate layers using linear classifier probes
Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
Transformer Feed-Forward Layers Are Key-Value Memories
